# Fuzzing Triage Report

## Crash analysis
No crash was observed: every one of the 23672 replays reports alive=1, and the per-iteration table is a byte-for-byte repeat of the same 28-frame template (4 families × 7 mutations) cycled ~845 times. Because the corpus is deterministic and the lengths never vary per (frame,mutation) pair (e.g., beacon/reserved_ie is always len=66, authentication/missing_mandatory always len=30, assoc_request/dup_ie always len=39), the campaign is effectively re-sending the same 28 packets — there is no value diversity within a mutation class, only repetition. The most structurally interesting stimuli that did NOT trip the target are: beacon/reserved_ie (len 56→66, +10B trailing unknown IE), beacon/dup_ie (+2B, likely duplicate SSID/Rates/DSSS), authentication/missing_mandatory (len 30, i.e., a 2-octet truncation of the fixed Auth Algorithm/Seq/Status block — the highest-value negative case for an auth state machine), and authentication/dup_ie (len 34, injecting an IE into a frame family that has no IEs per 802.11 except Challenge Text in Shared-Key seq 2/3). The fact that auth/missing_mandatory and auth/dup_ie both survived suggests the auth fixed-field parser is robust to short reads and tolerates trailing/unexpected IEs, but neither was varied in content, so we cannot conclude the parser is safe — only that this single byte pattern is. len_overflow and oversized_value never change the on-air length for any family (len stays equal to baseline: 56/30/32/37), which strongly implies those mutations are not actually altering the IE length field or value size on the wire — they are likely no-ops or are being normalized before transmit. That is why they produced zero signal and should be considered broken in the generator, not 'safe' on the target.

## Findings
- [info] campaign/no-crash: All 23672 replayed frames across beacon, probe_request, authentication, and assoc_request kept alive=1; no DoS or hang was observed in any of the 28-frame template cycles repeated through the run.
- [info] beacon/reserved_ie: Largest exercised frame at len=66 (baseline 56, +10 for an appended reserved IE). Target ingested every repetition without liveness loss, suggesting the beacon IE parser tolerates unknown Element IDs at end-of-frame.
- [info] beacon/dup_ie: len=58 duplicate-IE beacon variant (e.g., a second IE 0/1/3) accepted on every cycle; no observable effect on AP responsiveness.
- [info] beacon/missing_mandatory: len=54 beacon with a mandatory IE (SSID or Supported Rates) removed was tolerated repeatedly — parser appears to skip/short-circuit cleanly rather than fault.
- [info] probe_request/missing_mandatory: Shortest frame in the corpus (len=28, mandatory IE dropped from a 30-byte baseline) consistently kept the device alive; underflow path looks safe.
- [info] probe_request/reserved_ie: len=40 probe_request with appended reserved IE (baseline 30) caused no liveness change across all repetitions.
- [anomaly] authentication/dup_ie: authentication baseline is 32 bytes (Open System, seq 1, status 0, no IEs per spec), yet dup_ie produces len=34 — an IE is being injected into a frame family that has no mandatory IEs to duplicate. The target still stayed alive, but this mutation is semantically off-spec for auth and worth scrutinizing.
- [info] authentication/reserved_ie: len=42 auth frame (baseline 32 + 10-byte reserved IE) tolerated every cycle; auth state machine ignored the trailing unknown IE.
- [anomaly] authentication/missing_mandatory: len=30 (auth baseline 32 minus 2) suggests one of the fixed 2-octet fields (Algorithm, Seq, or Status) is being truncated — a fixed-field underflow, not an IE removal. Device remained alive across all reps, indicating the auth parser bounds-checks fixed fields.
- [info] assoc_request/reserved_ie: len=47 assoc_request (baseline 37 + reserved IE) accepted repeatedly; no impact on alive.
- [info] assoc_request/missing_mandatory: len=35 assoc_request (SSID or Supported Rates dropped) tolerated; association parser does not crash on missing mandatory IE.

## Next plan
{
  "focus_frames": [
    "authentication",
    "assoc_request",
    "beacon"
  ],
  "focus_mutations": [
    "missing_mandatory",
    "dup_ie",
    "reserved_ie",
    "len_overflow",
    "oversized_value"
  ],
  "drop_frames": [
    "probe_request"
  ],
  "rationale": "Three concrete pivots grounded in this summary: (1) Fix and re-prioritize len_overflow and oversized_value \u2014 across all 4 frame families their on-air length equals the baseline (beacon 56, probe_request 30, authentication 32, assoc_request 37), meaning the IE length-byte / value-size mutation is not actually emitted; this is the highest-leverage gap because length-field abuse on IEs (e.g., IE 0 SSID len=0xFF with no payload, IE 48 RSN len > frame remainder, IE 1 Rates len=0) is historically where 802.11 parsers fail. Once they actually mutate, re-run focused on beacon (IE 0/1/48/50) and assoc_request (IE 0/1/48/127). (2) Deepen authentication coverage: authentication/missing_mandatory currently truncates by exactly 2 bytes (len 30 vs 32) and authentication/dup_ie injects an IE into a frame that per spec carries none outside Shared-Key \u2014 vary which fixed field is truncated (Algorithm vs Seq vs Status), set Algorithm to 1 (Shared Key) or 3 (SAE) with seq=2/3 and include/omit IE 16 Challenge Text with extreme lengths, and inject IE 16 into Open-System auth where it is illegal. These are spec-grounded negative cases the agent has not yet generated value diversity for. (3) Drop probe_request from heavy rotation: it has only 2 mandatory IEs (0 SSID, 1 Supported Rates) and 1 optional (50), the smallest attack surface in the corpus, and every mutation (including missing_mandatory at len=28 and reserved_ie at len=40) kept alive=1 across ~845 repetitions \u2014 further budget there is wasted. Keep a minimal probe_request smoke set but reallocate cycles to beacon (richer IE set incl. TIM/Country/RSN) and assoc_request (RSN + Extended Capabilities)."
}

## Raw campaign summary

Frame families exercised: assoc_request=5915, authentication=5915, beacon=5922, probe_request=5920
Mutations exercised: baseline=3382, dup_ie=3382, len_overflow=3382, missing_mandatory=3382, oversized_value=3382, reserved_ie=3381, zero_len_ie=3381
No crash observed (target stayed alive for all frames).
